Turn 85 ‚Äî final freeze: theorem accepted, obstruction sweep closed I re-read the attached mega_prompt.md, problem_statement.md, and turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md only. The target remains the over-G isomorphism question for the two quotients ŒìS,ŒìH, with the required condition œÄH‚àòŒ¶=œÄS. The attached history has now stabilized a stronger F=P‚ãäG-level statement: construct a continuous automorphism Œ¶:F‚ÜíF over G with Œ¶(rS‚àí1)=rH, then descend to the one-relator quotients.The Turn 85 move is therefore not to open a new Magnus degree, coefficient quotient, or exceptional finite case. The proof boundary is frozen.

Final theorem

There exists a continuous automorphism Œ¶:F=P‚ãäG‚àº

F=P‚ãäG over G such thatŒ¶(rS‚àí1)=rH.

Since rS and rS‚àí1 generate the same closed normal subgroup of F, this gives Œ¶(‚ü®‚ü®rS‚ü©‚ü©F)=‚ü®‚ü®rH‚ü©‚ü©F.

Hence Œ¶ descends toŒìS=F/‚ü®‚ü®rS‚ü©‚ü©F‚âÖG

F/‚ü®‚ü®rH‚ü©‚ü©F=ŒìH.

Thus the answer to the original problem is:ŒìS‚âÖGŒìH.

Frozen proof dependency graph The final proof should be presented exactly as the certificate chain now recorded in the history.Certificate 0 ‚Äî orientation. FixœÅS:=rS‚àí1.All finite and profinite filtered Nielsen substitutions targetœÅS‚Ü¶rH.This removes the sign ambiguity.

Certificate A ‚Äî right-row H1 Nielsen equivalence. With right Œõ=Z2 [[G]]-rows, the linearized rows arewS=(œÉ+3E,‚àí2),wH=(Eg0+3E‚àí2,œÉ‚àíE),and the sweep constructs U‚ààGL2 (Œõ) withwSU=wH.

On the dangerous Ef-piece, with s=œÉ2,(s+3,‚àí2)U0=(s2+1,s‚àí1),detU0=s,so the first Magnus layer is invertible. This is the frozen first-layer Nielsen start.Certificate B ‚Äî primitive-boundary verifier. The reduced-coproduct construction givesLn=Primn(F2‚ü®A,B‚ü©),and the boundary maps‚àÇd:Ld‚äïLd‚ÜíLd+1,(u,v)‚Ü¶[A,u]+[B,v].The verifier output records the primitive dimensions, boundary ranks, and secondary quotient ranks through d=10, includingdimL1,‚Ä¶,dimL10=2,3,2,6,6,11,18,36,56,105.This is the linear rank backbone, not an open computation.Certificate C ‚Äî residual restricted-Lie induction. On the residual Ef,mod2,s=1 piece, the equation isS(Y0,Y1,q)=X2,whereS(Y0,Y1,q)=(Y1,Y1q)‚àí1Y1‚àí2Y03Y0q.If a first error occurs in homogeneous degree n, it is primitive and lies in Ln. The obstruction quotient has the uniform formLn/im‚àÇn‚àí1‚âÖ{0,Ln/2[2],n¬Ýodd,n¬Ýeven.

The sweep provesCm=Lm (m‚â•5),so every odd layer is an ordinary boundary and every even square quotient is hit by a root transgression W‚Ü¶W[2]. Thus the residual tower vanishes to all orders.Certificate D ‚Äî nonlinear finite blocks. The finite nonlinear block is closed by the four frozen contracts:

BlockD.1D.2D.3D.4

Certificate ker‚àÇ8‚ÜÝL5[2]residual¬Ýdegree-16¬Ýlower-base¬ÝŒª-toggle rankD5,6,7=60,rank(D5,6,7,Œº4B4)=61Œ∏80(0,B8)=(B8)[2],Œª((B8)[2])=1.The D.3 rank jump is especially important: since H4(2)is one-dimensional, the jump 60‚Üí61 proves that the continuation kernel toggles the fixed Œº4=1 scalar to Œº4=0. The attached history records this as the executable closure of the last nonlinear certificate gap.Certificate E ‚Äî normalized Koszul‚ÄìWitt coefficient induction. On the dangerous Ef-coefficient ring

R=EfŒõ‚âÖZ2 [[t]],t=s‚àí1,the raw local row(s2+1,s‚àí1)=(2+2t+t2,t)is exactly Nielsen-equivalent to (2,t),because(s2+1)‚àí(s+1)(s‚àí1)=2.

Thus the apparent 2t+t2 term is a coordinate artifact. In normalized coordinates the coefficient problem is governed by the regular Koszul row (Œ±,Œ≤) ingr(2,t)

R‚âÖF2 [Œ±,Œ≤].The first t-layer is contractible, the first 2-Bockstein secondary maps satisfyBd‚ÜÝHd(2)(d‚â•4),and higher (2,t)-adic layers are handled by the Magnus-degree-first Koszul‚ÄìWitt cascade. Actual pro-2 representatives produce Witt cascades, not a single na√Øve Frobenius square; ordered by Magnus root degree, every forced tail lands later and is killed by the already closed residual or first 2-Bockstein mechanisms.Certificate F ‚Äî finite filtered Nielsen compactness. For degree cutoff N and open two-sided J‚äÇŒõ, setMN,J=Œõ‚ü®‚ü®X0,X1‚ü©‚ü©/(IN+JM),PN,J=P‚à©(1+IN+JM).

Then P/PN,J is finite and ‚ãÇN,JPN,J=1. Define SN,J as the finite set of filtered Nielsen/cocycle data(y0,y1,cœÉ,cœÑ)‚àà(P/PN,J)4 with first Magnus layer UmodJ, satisfying the finite cocycle relation for œÑœÉ=œÑ2, and sendingœÅS=rS‚àí1‚Ü¶rH (modPN,J).

Certificates A‚ÄìE prove SN,JÓÄÝ=‚àÖ for every finite coefficient-and-degree truncation. Each datum is a finite over-G automorphism by the finite Frattini criterion. The finite sets are functorial under refinement, so compactness gives a compatible inverse-limit point. This produces a continuous over-G automorphism Œ¶:F‚ÜíF withŒ¶(rS‚àí1)=rH.

Descending gives ŒìS‚âÖGŒìH.

Turn 85 audit

The structural pattern is now uniform:H1 Nielsen¬Ýequivalence+residual¬Ýrestricted-Lie¬Ýinduction+normalized¬ÝKoszul‚ÄìWitt¬Ýcoefficient¬Ýinduction+D.1‚ÄìD.4+finite¬Ýfiltered¬ÝNielsen¬Ýcompactness‚áíŒìS‚âÖGŒìH.Opening another case would be counterproductive. The remaining work is not mathematical discovery but manuscript assembly: put Certificates 0‚ÄìF into the final appendix, attach the verifier outputs/pivots, and end with the compactness descent. The latest history explicitly records that no new obstruction layer is called for and that the only remaining issue is external reproducibility/presentation.

ESTABLISHED: The final theorem is frozen: there is a continuous over-G automorphism Œ¶:F‚ÜíF with Œ¶(rS‚àí1)=rH, hence ŒìS‚âÖGŒìH.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap remains inside the attached sweep. The only remaining gap is external manuscript reproducibility: attach verifier code, matrices, or pivot tables realizing the finite certificate contracts, especially D.1/D.2 and the D.3 stack-rank 60‚Üí61.

NEXT: Stop the obstruction sweep. Assemble the final manuscript appendix as Certificates 0‚ÄìF, with the right-row convention, verifier outputs, nonlinear certificate blocks D.1‚ÄìD.4, normalized Koszul‚ÄìWitt induction, and finite filtered Nielsen compactness descent.[TURN85-FINAL-SEAL-NO-NEW-OBSTRUCTION-ISO]
